meta.context: willow-2.0 sandbox — grove-only, no Willow MCP
meta.agent: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (no identity in this sandbox)
meta.purpose: Design session — ledger app suite architecture. No production builds. Three worktrees created.

decisions.decision: Build a Textual TUI personal budget app in willow-2.0/ledger/
decisions.rationale: Local-first, yggdrasil:v9 for NL entry + categorization + insights, SQLite via WILLOW_20_DB
decisions.status: BUILT — core app complete, feature backlog in feature_cards.json

decisions.decision: Two-stage file ingestion: Global Scout (watches ~/Downloads) → App Inbox (~/.willow/ledger-inbox/)
decisions.rationale: App should never watch communal folders directly. Scout classifies, annotates, routes. App receives file + sidecar.
decisions.status: DESIGNED — not yet built. Cards: inbox-watcher, csv-statement-parser, pdf-statement-parser, ofx-parser, dedup-engine, bank-format-library, sidecar-metadata, scout-notification, scout-config

decisions.decision: Scout is bidirectional — inbound (statements → apps) AND outbound (preference atoms → KB outbox)
decisions.rationale: Local Textual apps have no MCP access. Consent gate writes atom JSON to ~/.willow/kb-outbox/. Scout delivers to willow_knowledge_ingest. No new infrastructure needed.
decisions.status: DESIGNED — architectural insight from this session, not yet built

decisions.decision: Three-app suite: personal ledger, public ledger TUI (Vor), corporate ledger (Gjald)
decisions.rationale: Three scales of the same financial intelligence graph. Same entity layer. Same local model. Different interfaces.
decisions.status: Personal = built. Public TUI = worktree created (wt-vor-tui), not built. Corporate = worktree created (wt-corp-ledger-tui), sketched only.

decisions.decision: Unified vision: public ledger (Vor) doubles as investment research engine fed by USASpending + ProPublica
decisions.rationale: User forms opinion from public record, asks yggdrasil for investment take given personal position + business exposure + stated values. Sovereign due diligence with no external subscription.

decisions.decision: Values filters are first-class, binary-presence not percentage-threshold
decisions.rationale: Percentage sliders let almost everyone through. Sean's DoD filter = any direct contract = flagged, no threshold. The system respects that distinction.
decisions.status: DESIGNED — canonical example captured in investment-intelligence card

decisions.decision: Consent gate: two-stream KB extraction — personal preferences (consent gated) vs factual entity knowledge (shared, no gate)
decisions.rationale: Public data (DoD contractor list, 990 data, entity graph) belongs in shared KB. User values/filters are personal atoms requiring explicit consent before leaving local scope.
decisions.status: DESIGNED — architectural pattern, not yet built

decisions.decision: Naming deferred — full Norse shortlist captured, mapping resolves when full app ecosystem is laid out
decisions.rationale: Names may map to apps, sub-features, or sub-components. Can't decide in isolation.

corporate_ledger.concept: Double-entry bookkeeping, chart of accounts, P&L, multi-entity (in-house or external accountant)
corporate_ledger.key_feature: NL journal entry — describe transaction, yggdrasil generates double-entry with account codes, accountant reviews

entity_graph.description: Shared node layer across all three apps. Entity resolution normalizes 'Amazon', 'AMZN', 'AWS', 'Amazon.com Inc' to one node.

scout.description: Standalone background process. Watches configured folders. Classifies files. Routes with sidecar. Bidirectional: inbound (statements → app inboxes) + outbound (preference atoms → kb-outbox).
scout.learns: Pattern library grows — repeat imports from same bank skip LLM entirely

sidecar.description: JSON companion file written alongside routed financial statement. Carries pre-computed classification so app skips redundant analysis.

consent_gate.description: UX pattern in any app. When user sets durable preference, app asks: remember across all Willow apps? If yes, writes atom JSON to ~/.willow/kb-outbox/. Scout delivers.

two_streams.personal_preferences: consent required, written to user KB namespace
two_streams.factual_entity_knowledge: no consent needed (public data), written to shared KB namespace

consent_gate.threshold_rule: What counts as significant is always the user's call. System stores the rule, never overrides it.

investment_intelligence.description: Cross-app synthesis layer. Vor surfaces company → yggdrasil reads public record + personal position + business exposure → investment opinion.

canonical_example.filter: Any tech company with direct DoD contract = flagged. Binary. No percentage threshold.
canonical_example.secondary_check: Personal spending data checked first — user likely already paying flagged entities via subscriptions before investment question is reached
canonical_example.model_output: Flagged holdings + flagged monthly spend + alternatives with no defense contracts + exit option

kb_extraction_pending.note: willow_knowledge_ingest not available in willow-2.0 sandbox. Atoms written to willow-2.0.db records table as local store. Full KB ingest requires Hanuman session with Willow MCP.

open_questions: Which app gets built first when construction begins?
Does the scout live in willow-2.0 or its own repo?
Does the entity graph live in willow-2.0.db or a separate shared DB accessible to all three apps?
Name mapping — which Norse name goes to which app or layer?
PR threshold — what does 'worthy of a new repo' look like for each app?
next_session_first_bite: Pick one of the three apps and build the TUI. Recommended: Vor (public ledger TUI) — backend already exists at safe-app-public-ledger, TUI is the only missing piece.
